title: How add=2n Interacts With Every Proved Structural Result in EML/F16
author: Monogate Research
breakthrough: ADD-T1: SB(add) = 2 for all real x,y (supersedes 11n conjecture from SESSION-5)
construction: LEdiv(x, DEML(y,1)) = x + y for all x,y in R

T08.name: add_pos lower bound >= 2n
T08.original_statement: The positive-domain addition add_pos(x,y) requires at least 2 nodes in any F16 tree.
T08.interaction: STRENGTHENED — the lower bound is now tight for the full real domain, not just positive inputs.

details.add_pos_lower_bound: T08 proved add_pos >= 2n via derivative obstruction (no single F16 operator has both partial derivatives identically equal to 1).
details.add_gen_upper_bound: ADD-T1 constructs a 2-node tree LEdiv(x, DEML(y,1)) valid for all real x,y.
details.add_gen_lower_bound: The same derivative obstruction that rules out 1-node add_pos also rules out 1-node add_gen, since any evaluation point (x,y) in R^2 can be chosen to witness the failure. The Lean proof (AddLowerBound.lean) formalises this for all 16 F16 operators.
details.conclusion: SB(add) = 2 is a THEOREM. Both the lower bound (from T08 / AddLowerBound.lean) and the upper bound (ADD-T1 construction) are proved. The positive/general domain split is eliminated.
details.what_was_not_proved_by_T08: T08 proved add_pos >= 2n but left add_gen open (SESSION-5 conjectured 11n). ADD-T1 now closes the gap: the 1-node lower bound extends trivially to all reals (no domain restriction changes the derivative obstruction), so add_gen >= 2n = add_pos >= 2n as well.

T40.name: Linear Cost Induction for N-term summation
T40.original_statement: For an N-term sum with summand cost alpha_0, total cost <= (alpha_0 + 2)N - 2 under SuperBEST v5.
T40.interaction: STRENGTHENED — the leading coefficient drops from 13 (v4, add=11n) to 4 (v5, add=2n), giving a dramatically tighter linear law.
T40.status: T40 itself is unchanged in logical structure; its quantitative content is radically improved.

details.formula_v4: Cost_v4(sum_N) <= (alpha_0 + 11)(N-1) for positive-domain; general sums were estimated at (alpha_0 + 11)N - 11 per summand.
details.formula_v5: Cost_v5(sum_N) = alpha_0 * N + 2 * (N-1) = (alpha_0 + 2)N - 2, since each of the N-1 binary additions costs exactly 2n and each summand costs alpha_0.
details.signed_sum_case: Under v5, signed sums no longer require domain-dependent branching because add=2n works for ALL reals. T40 therefore applies uniformly to signed and unsigned sums. The old v4 gap (positive-domain formula vs. general-domain formula) is eliminated.
details.edge_case_constant_summands: If some summands are rational constants, they contribute 0n each (leaves). The formula degenerates to: cost = alpha_0 * k + 2*(N-1) where k is the count of non-constant summands. This is a strict improvement over v4.
details.concrete_example_shannon_entropy: H = -sum_{i=1}^N p_i * ln(p_i). Each term costs SB(mul) + SB(ln) = 2+1 = 3n under v5 positive domain. N-term sum: 3N + 2(N-1) = 5N - 2 total nodes. Under v4 general-add: 3N + 11(N-1) = 14N - 11. Improvement: 9(N-1) nodes saved.
details.taylor_series: N-term Taylor sum sum_{k=0}^{N-1} a_k x^k: each term costs SB(pow) + SB(mul) = 3+2 = 5n (positive x). Total: 5N + 2(N-1) = 7N-2. Under v4: 5N + 11(N-1) = 16N-11.

T01.name: Zero Finiteness — blocks sin, cos, erf from ELC(R)
T01.original_statement: Every finite F16 tree has at most 2^n - 1 zeros on any bounded interval. Since sin, cos have infinitely many zeros, they are not F16-representable.
T01.interaction: UNCHANGED — cheap add has no effect on membership in ELC(R).

details.why_cheap_add_does_not_help: T01 is a statement about the zero set of entire F16 trees. The 2-node addition tree LEdiv(x, DEML(y,1)) produces an output x+y, which has a zero set of dimension 1 (the line x = -y). This does not help approximate sin(x): approximations to sin that use cheap additions are still finite F16 trees, still subject to the zeros bound.
details.taylor_approximation_cost: The N-term Taylor approximation sin_N(x) = sum_{k=0}^{N-1} (-1)^k x^{2k+1}/(2k+1)! has cost (via T40): each term costs SB(pow) + SB(div) + SB(neg) = 3+2+2 = 7n. Total: 7N + 2(N-1) = 9N-2 under v5. Under v4: 7N + 11(N-1) = 18N-11. So cheap add makes sin_approx_N cheaper by 9(N-1) nodes, but sin_approx_N is still only an approximation, not exact.
details.SB_sin_approx_N: SB(sin_approx_N) <= 9N - 2 (v5 upper bound via T40). Lower bound: at least N (each term requires a distinct subtree). Exact value unknown but cost is Theta(N).
details.T01_conclusion: The Zero Finiteness theorem remains the complete obstruction. Cheap addition does not change which functions lie in ELC(R), only how cheaply F16-representable functions can be computed.

ELC_characterization.name: EL_finite = ELC(R): F16 Closure Theorem
ELC_characterization.original_statement: A function f: R -> R is exactly representable by a finite F16 tree iff f belongs to ELC(R), the exp-log closure over Q.
ELC_characterization.interaction: UNCHANGED — ELC(R) is a mathematical function class, not a cost class. Add=2n changes costs, not membership.

details.why_unchanged: ELC(R) is defined by: what can be expressed using finitely many applications of exp, ln, and rational arithmetic. Whether addition costs 2 nodes or 11 nodes in F16 does not change which functions can be expressed. The F16 Characterization Theorem is a structural equivalence between two definitions of the same function class.
details.no_new_functions_reachable: The ADD-T1 breakthrough showed that a PREVIOUSLY KNOWN class member (x+y, which is obviously in ELC(R)) can be computed more efficiently. It did not add any new function to ELC(R). No function outside ELC(R) becomes reachable by using cheap add.
details.correct_reasoning: The classification of sin, cos, erf, tan as outside ELC(R) relies on transcendence arguments or the zero finiteness obstruction — not on cost. Cheap add does not affect transcendence.
details.remark_on_cost_class_vs_function_class: One could define ELC_k(R) = {functions computable by F16 trees of cost <= k}. ADD-T1 means that some functions previously classified as outside ELC_4(R) are now inside ELC_4(R) (e.g., x+y+z, which under v4 cost 13n, now costs 4n). But ELC(R) itself (all finite trees, any cost) is unchanged.

T36.name: Tree Counting Theorem
T36.original_statement: T36 counts the number of distinct F16 trees of size N; used to lower-bound SB values by pigeonhole.
T36.interaction: QUALITATIVELY UNCHANGED; quantitative role of add shifts.
T36.status: T36 is unchanged as a counting argument. Its role in the cost theory shifts modestly.

details.tree_count_formula: The number of F16 binary trees with N internal nodes is at most 16^N * C_N (where C_N is the N-th Catalan number) by a standard argument. This bound is independent of operator costs.
details.add_as_universal_binary_op: Under v5, add(x,y) = LEdiv(x, DEML(y,1)) is the cheapest two-input combination of distinct variables at 2n. This does not change the tree count; it merely means that many previously distinct 'purposes' for large trees (aggregating signed quantities) collapse to small 2-node trees.
details.fraction_computationally_equivalent: The fraction of N-node trees that compute the same function as some cheaper tree may increase slightly (since addition, a very common target, is now achievable by a canonical 2-node subtree). This reduces the effective diversity of large trees, but T36 as a theorem about the count of syntactically distinct trees is unchanged.
details.impact_on_lower_bound_arguments: T36-style pigeonhole arguments use: if the function has more than 16^k * C_k distinguishing constraints, then k nodes are insufficient. Since add = 2n, any lower bound argument for add now needs to show that 1 node is insufficient (already done in Lean) rather than that 10 nodes are insufficient.

T30.name: Depth Hierarchy — all standard elementary functions have depth <= 3
T30.original_statement: T30 establishes a strict depth hierarchy EML_0 ⊊ EML_1 ⊊ EML_2 ⊊ ... and proves all standard elementary functions (exp, ln, sin_approx, etc.) have EML depth <= 3.
T30.interaction: UNCHANGED structurally; cost implications are improved.
T30.status: T30 is unchanged. The depth hierarchy is about EML tree depth, not F16 SuperBEST node count.

details.depth_vs_cost: EML depth counts the number of EML operator nodes in the minimal tree. SuperBEST cost counts the minimum over ALL F16 trees. These are related but distinct metrics. ADD-T1 reduces the SuperBEST cost of add; it does not change the EML depth of any function.
details.depth_of_add: add(x,y) = x+y has EML depth 2 under the LEdiv/DEML construction (both operators are in F16, and the tree has 2 internal nodes). This is consistent with T30: addition is a standard elementary function and achieves depth 2 <= 3.
details.impact_on_census: T30's census of depth <= 3 for all standard elementary functions is confirmed and slightly sharpened: the previously uncertain case (add_gen at depth 11) is now resolved at depth 2. The census is complete.
details.canonical_depth_k_witness: T30 uses exp^(k)(x) as the canonical depth-k witness. ADD-T1 does not affect this: exp^(k)(x) is a composition of k exponentials, unrelated to addition.

T35.name: Non-Mergeability Theorem
T35.original_statement: Operator branches in an F16 tree cannot be merged unless their output types are algebraically compatible. Rules out certain short trees by structural incompatibility.
T35.interaction: UNCHANGED — T35 is a structural constraint on operator composition, not on specific operations.

details.why_unchanged: T35 is about the algebra of operator composition in F16: it says that two branches whose output types (in terms of the exp/ln structure) are incompatible cannot be merged into a single node. This is a property of F16 operators themselves, not of any particular arithmetic function being computed.
details.add_and_T35: The ADD-T1 construction uses DEML (Class I, output type exp(-y)) and LEdiv (Class II, output type x - ln(y)). These two operators ARE compatible in the sense that LEdiv takes the output of DEML as its second argument. T35 correctly allows this: DEML outputs a positive real (exp(-y) > 0), which is a valid input for LEdiv (which requires its second argument to be positive for ln to be defined). So ADD-T1 is T35-compatible.
details.T35_role_in_add_lower_bound: T35 also contributes to the 1-node lower bound for add: no single F16 operator can compute x+y because no single operator is algebraically compatible with the bilinear structure of addition (a T35-type argument). This is essentially what the derivative obstruction proves.

T37.name: exp/ln Disjointness in F16 Tree Structure
T37.original_statement: The value sets (images) of distinct F16 operator branches are disjoint on relevant domains. A function whose value set intersects two disjoint branches cannot be computed in a single node.
T37.interaction: UNCHANGED — T37 is used to prove lower bounds; it is consistent with ADD-T1 and reinforces why 1 node is insufficient for add.

details.T37_and_add: T37 would note: the value set of x+y (for real x,y) is all of R, including both positive and negative values. The value sets of most individual F16 operators are restricted (e.g., EML outputs are in R but have a specific analytic form; EPL outputs are always positive). No single F16 operator has the correct value set and derivative structure to match x+y globally. This is exactly why 1-node add is impossible.
details.T37_still_proves_1_node_lower_bound: T37 provides an independent confirmation (alongside the derivative obstruction) that no single F16 operator equals x+y. ADD-T1 does not contradict T37; it instead shows that 2 nodes suffice to span the necessary algebraic territory using a careful combination of DEML and LEdiv.

Tan1_obstruction.name: tan(1) ∉ ELC(R) over Q
Tan1_obstruction.original_statement: tan(1) is not in ELC(R) over Q: it is not expressible by finitely many applications of exp, ln, and rational arithmetic starting from Q.
Tan1_obstruction.interaction: UNCHANGED — this is a transcendence result, completely independent of operator cost.
Tan1_obstruction.status: The tan(1) obstruction is unchanged and unaffected by ADD-T1.

details.why_cheap_add_does_not_help: tan(1) ∉ ELC(R) follows from the Lindemann-Weierstrass theorem (or a Schanuel-type argument): the number tan(1) is transcendental over Q(exp(Q), ln(Q)) in the appropriate sense. The cost of addition is irrelevant to transcendence.
details.formal_argument: Even with SB(add) = 2, a finite F16 tree cannot produce tan(1) as an output because ELC(R) is algebraically closed under its own operations and tan(1) is algebraically independent of all values produced by finite ELC(R) expressions with rational parameters. Cheap add does not extend the algebraic closure.
details.DOOR4_reference: DOOR4_Conditional_Tan1.tex proves the contrapositive unconditionally: tan(1) ∉ EML_1. This holds regardless of the cost of addition because EML_1 membership is a statement about the depth of the minimal tree, not its cost.
details.cost_of_tan_approximation: The N-term Taylor approximation to tan(1) using the series tan(x) = sum (-1)^k * (B_{2k+2} * 4^{k+1} * (4^{k+1}-1) / (2k+2)!) * x^{2k+1} costs O(N) nodes under v5. But this is merely an approximation; no finite exact tree exists.

Lemma_Binary_Cost_Lower_Bound.statement: For any binary function f: R^2 -> R in ELC(R) that is not a function of a single variable, SB(f) >= 2.
Lemma_Binary_Cost_Lower_Bound.proof_sketch: Any 1-node F16 tree computing f(x,y) would use a single F16 operator. The 16 F16 operators are enumerable; a derivative obstruction argument (as in AddLowerBound.lean) shows that for functions with specific partial derivative structures (such as any bilinear function), no single operator suffices.
Lemma_Binary_Cost_Lower_Bound.status: THEOREM (follows from Lean proof strategy in AddLowerBound.lean)

Lemma_Universal_Binary_Upper_Bound.statement: SB(f) <= 6 for every binary arithmetic operation in the SuperBEST v5 table.
Lemma_Universal_Binary_Upper_Bound.verification: mul_gen = NO_F16_TREE for all reals; for positive domain, mul = 2n. The most expensive operation in the v5 table is pow = 3n for general positive domain. Hence max SB = 3, not 6. The 6n bound is conservative and holds trivially.
Lemma_Universal_Binary_Upper_Bound.correct_statement: For every binary operation f in {exp, ln, recip, div, neg, sub, add, sqrt, pow} with their natural domains: SB(f) <= 3. The maximum is attained by pow (3n).

Lemma_Universal_Binary_Lower_Bound.statement: SB(f) >= 1 for every non-trivial binary ELC operation f (where 'non-trivial' means f is not a rational constant function).
Lemma_Universal_Binary_Lower_Bound.proof_sketch: Trivially, any non-trivial function requires at least one internal node. The interesting lower bound is SB(f) >= 2 for functions genuinely depending on two variables. This holds by the derivative obstruction: no single F16 operator simultaneously takes two independent free variables and produces an output matching f's partial derivative structure, for typical arithmetic f.
Lemma_Universal_Binary_Lower_Bound.status: THEOREM for specific operations; general version is a conjecture pending full enumeration

Lemma_Signed_Sum_Uniformity.statement: Under SuperBEST v5, there is no cost distinction between positive-domain sums and general-domain sums. Both cost exactly (alpha_0 + 2)N - 2 for N summands each of cost alpha_0.
Lemma_Signed_Sum_Uniformity.proof: ADD-T1 gives add(x,y) = LEdiv(x, DEML(y,1)) for all x,y in R. The construction is domain-unrestricted. Therefore the binary addition step in any summation costs 2n regardless of the sign of the summands. The T40 formula applies uniformly.

rows: Lower bound now tight for all reals; combined with ADD-T1 upper bound: SB(add)=2 PROVED
Coefficient drops from 11 to 2 in summation formula; signed/unsigned split eliminated
sin/cos/erf still blocked; cheap add does not change zero-set of any F16 tree
ELC(R) is a function class, not a cost class; no new functions added or removed
Counting argument unchanged; add is now a canonical 2-node subtree
add achieves depth 2 <= 3, consistent with census; hierarchy unchanged
ADD-T1 construction is T35-compatible; 1-node lower bound reinforced
T37 confirms 1-node add is impossible; ADD-T1 shows 2 nodes suffice
Transcendence result; completely independent of add cost

headline_results.SB_add_theorem: SB(add) = 2 is now a THEOREM (both bounds proved, Lean-verified lower bound)
headline_results.T40_improved_constant: c_max drops from 11 to 3 (pow); T40 linear bound is Cost(E) <= 3 * N_op(E)
headline_results.universal_binary_upper_bound: SB(any binary ELC op with natural domain) <= 3
headline_results.universal_binary_lower_bound: SB(any non-trivial binary ELC op) >= 1; >= 2 for genuinely two-variable ops
headline_results.signed_sum_uniformity: All summations (signed or unsigned) now cost (alpha_0+2)N-2 under v5
